title: Motor and Sensory Regions of the Cerebral Cortex
description: OpenStax illustration of the lateral surface of the brain, highlighting the functional areas of the cerebral cortex. It distinguishes the primary motor cortex in the frontal lobe from the primary somatosensory cortex in the parietal lobe (separated by the central sulcus), alongside critical language centers.
clinical_notes: Middle Cerebral Artery (MCA) strokes commonly affect the lateral cortical surface shown here. A left MCA infarct often results in right-sided hemiparesis (affecting the primary motor cortex) and expressive/receptive aphasia (Broca's/Wernicke's areas). OT intervention focuses on neuro-reeducation, constraint-induced movement therapy (CIMT) for the upper extremity, and adapting ADLs to accommodate communication deficits.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 14 – The Brain and Cranial Nerves
